Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-25-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Smith, Esq., 415.294.1465, for plaintiff Richard Devine; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ROSALBA NAVARRO'S Motion To Strike Portions Of First Amended Complaint is granted. No opposition filed. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-25-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Smith, Esq., 415.294.1465, for plaintiff Richard Devine; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ROSALBA NAVARRO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. As to the statute of limitations argument, the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") alleges that payment on the loan was due when Defendant "was [financially] able to do so." (FAC, par. 12.) The FAC alleges that Defendant was financially able to do so in June 2013. (FAC, par. 14.) The two-year statute of limitations began running in June 2013, or when this condition precedent was met. The complaint was filed in January 2014, and is therefore timely. As to the statute of frauds argument, the one year provision "applies to those contracts which, by their terms, cannot possibly be performed within one year." (White Lighting Co. v. Wolf (1968) 68 Cal.2d 336, 343.) As pled, this contract could have been performed within one year. Further, this was a contract between two individuals without any third party guaranty. As such, Civ. Code sec. 1624(a)(2) does not bar the claim. Lastly, the FAC is not so uncertain that Defendant cannot reasonably respond. (See Khoury v. Maly's of Calif., Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616; Weil & Brown, Cal. Prac. Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2013) par. 7:85.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories, Set One
